title: Ownership, Borrowing, Aliasing, and API Contracts · Changed-Lifetime Lab
summary: An independent maintainer ports the ownership-annotated buffer API to a changed target with tighter memory, different input shape, injected allocation failure, and no worked solution. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of ownership-annotated buffer API with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Classify each pointer as unique owner, shared owner, mutable borrow, immutable borrow, weak observation, or non-owning handle.
Make ownership transfer conditional on observable success and leave failure states unambiguous.
Bound every borrow by owner lifetime, mutation rules, callback duration, and asynchronous retention.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Unique Shared Borrowed
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab native-systems team handles memory case 5524. Produce bounded C evidence to track owner, borrow, mutation, and release state; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Lab Ownership Table Review
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Ownership Table Review: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A lab native-systems team handles memory case 7713. Produce bounded C evidence to track owner, borrow, mutation, and release state; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Review public APIs with an ownership table covering inputs, outputs, globals, nested allocations, and cleanup.
